title: How Parallax Measures Star Distance
script_summary: Exploring how stellar parallax helps us measure distances to nearby stars.

segments.voiceover: Nearby stars shift as Earth moves—this shift reveals their distance.
This phenomenon is called stellar parallax. It's all about perspective.
As Earth orbits the Sun, stars appear to slightly shift against the background.
Using trigonometry, astronomers calculate distances based on this tiny shift.
This method allows us to measure stars thousands of light-years away!
